summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer git identity and OS username to undisclosed third-party host
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js) reads the installer's ~/.gitconfig via iniparser and os.userInfo(), then issues an HTTPS GET to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext with the installer's OS username, git user.name, and git user.email as query parameters. The destination domain does not match the package's declared publisher homepage, and no README / documentation discloses this telemetry. A special-case branch routes traffic to localhost:1962 when the OS username matches the author's own handle, confirming 2tak.l.serverhost.name is the author-controlled remote collection endpoint. The postinstall script then rewrites the installed package.json to strip the scripts/devDependencies keys and deletes dist/scripts and dist/data — anti-forensic self-cleanup that removes the preinstall code from the installed tree after it has run, so a developer inspecting node_modules after install will not see the phone-home code. Installer harm is concrete: identity data is exfiltrated without consent at npm install time, and evidence is then erased.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
